routine.title: Parent-and-tot swim
routine.stage_fit_rationale: Water comfort at the toddler stage is best built through short, caregiver-supported sessions — the parent-in-water format matches exactly what this age needs to feel safe and begin splashing confidently. It also directly feeds this child's stated interest in water.

routine.title: Neighbourhood park and playground
routine.stage_fit_rationale: Toddlers build gross-motor skills and spatial confidence through unstructured outdoor movement — no fixed schedule means this can flex around nap windows, which is essential at this stage. It also channels this child's love of movement in the most open-ended way possible.

parsed.rationale: Two well-spaced anchor points — a structured water session mid-week and a flexible outdoor outing later in the week — give enough rhythm for a toddler to anticipate while leaving plenty of room for rest and spontaneity.
parsed.notes: Toddler music-and-movement was left out not because it's a poor fit (it's well-pitched for the stage) but because three items risks over-scheduling a toddler week. If the park outing ever needs to be skipped, music-and-movement is a ready swap-in for Thursday — same movement interest, indoors, short format.
